description_en: The branch of science that deals with celestial objects, space, and the physical universe as a whole.
example: His interest in astronomy began when he received a telescope for his birthday.

description_en: The study of living organisms, divided into many specialized fields.
example: Molecular biology explores the fundamental building blocks of life.

description_en: A substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without itself undergoing any permanent chemical change.
example: The enzyme acts as a biological catalyst in the digestion process.

description_en: A measurable extent of some kind, such as length, breadth, depth, or height.
example: The scientists are exploring the possibility of other dimensions beyond our own.

description_en: The process by which different kinds of living organisms are thought to have developed and diversified from earlier forms.
example: Darwin's theory of evolution changed our understanding of life on Earth.

description_en: The remains or impression of a prehistoric organism preserved in petrified form or as a mold or cast in rock.
example: The archaeologists discovered a dinosaur fossil in the desert.

description_en: The force that attracts a body toward the center of the earth, or toward any other physical body having mass.
example: Gravity is the force that keeps the planets in orbit around the sun.

description_en: A proposed explanation made on the basis of limited evidence as a starting point for further investigation.
example: The research team developed a hypothesis to explain the strange data.

description_en: Each of two or more forms of the same element that contain equal numbers of protons but different numbers of neutrons.
example: Carbon-14 is a radioactive isotope used for dating old objects.

meaning: t·∫°p ch√≠ khoa h·ªçc; nh·∫≠t k√Ω
description_en: A newspaper or magazine that deals with a particular subject or professional activity.
example: The study was published in the world's leading medical journal.

description_en: Relating to or resulting from motion.
example: The car converts chemical energy into kinetic energy to move.

description_en: A room or building equipped for scientific experiments, research, or teaching.
example: The new lab has state-of-the-art equipment for genetic research.

description_en: A group of atoms bonded together, representing the smallest fundamental unit of a chemical compound.
example: A water molecule consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

description_en: A cloud of gas and dust in outer space, visible in the night sky as an indistinct bright patch or as a dark silhouette.
example: The Orion Nebula is a famous star-forming region.

description_en: An individual animal, plant, or single-celled life form.
example: Thousands of tiny organisms live in a single drop of pond water.

description_en: The branch of science concerned with the nature and properties of matter and energy.
example: Physics helps us understand how the universe works at a fundamental level.

description_en: A discrete quantity of energy proportional in magnitude to the frequency of the radiation it represents.
example: Quantum mechanics explores the behavior of matter at the atomic level.

description_en: The emission of energy as electromagnetic waves or as moving subatomic particles.
example: Too Much exposure to ultraviolet radiation can damage the skin.

description_en: Imitation of a situation or process.
example: Scientists use computer simulations to model climate change.
note: Using models to study real-world scenarios.

description_en: A system of ideas intended to explain something.
example: Einstein's theory of relativity revolutionized the field of physics.

description_en: All existing matter and space considered as a whole.
example: The universe is estimated to be around 13.8 billion years old.

description_en: The speed of something in a given direction.
example: The escape velocity needed to leave Earth is very high.

description_en: The distance between successive crests of a wave.
example: Red light has a longer wavelength than blue light.
note: Measure for light and sound waves.

description_en: A heavy, colorless, odorless noble gas.
example: Xenon is used in some specialized high-intensity lamps.

description_en: The amount of an agricultural or industrial product.
example: The chemical reaction yielded a high amount of pure substance.

description_en: The scientific study of animals.
example: Zoology is essential for understanding wildlife conservation.

description_en: A vehicle's capacity to gain speed within a short time.
example: The rocket's acceleration was incredible during launch.

description_en: The exploitation of biological processes for industrial and other purposes.
example: Biotechnology is used to develop new medicines and vaccines.

description_en: A distinct compound or substance, especially one which has been artificially prepared or purified.
example: Be careful when handling hazardous chemicals in the lab.

description_en: The action or process of discovering or being discovered.
example: The discovery of penicillin was a major breakthrough in medicine.

description_en: A scientific procedure undertaken to make a discovery, test a hypothesis, or demonstrate a known fact.
example: We conducted an experiment to test the effects of heat on the metal.

description_en: A substance that has no fixed shape and yields easily to external pressure.
example: Water and air are both classified as fluids in physics.

description_en: The study of heredity and the variation of inherited characteristics.
example: Genetics plays a huge role in determining our physical traits.

description_en: A three-dimensional image formed by the interference of light beams from a laser or other coherent light source.
example: The museum uses holograms to display fragile artifacts.

description_en: A tendency to do nothing or to remain unchanged.
example: The heavy ball has more inertia than the light one.

description_en: The largest planet in our solar system.
example: Jupiter is a gas giant with a famous Great Red Spot.
note: The fifth planet from the sun.

description_en: A unit of power equal to 1,000 watts.
example: The new solar panels can generate up to 5 kilowatts of power.

description_en: Reasoning conducted or assessed according to strict principles of validity.
example: Scientific research must follow the rules of formal logic.

description_en: An optical instrument used for viewing very small objects.
example: Looking through a microscope revealed a world of tiny bacteria.
note: Used to see things invisible to the naked eye.

description_en: A subatomic particle of about the same mass as a proton but without an electric charge.
example: The nucleus of an atom consists of protons and neutrons.

description_en: The action or process of observing something or someone carefully in order to gain information.
example: Careful observation is the first step in the scientific method.
note: Gathering data through senses or instruments.

description_en: A fact or situation that is observed to exist or happen, especially one whose cause or explanation is in question.
example: The Northern Lights are a spectacular natural phenomenon.

description_en: Any of a number of subatomic particles carrying a fractional electric charge.
example: Protons and neutrons are made up of even smaller particles called quarks.

description_en: A process that involves rearrangement of the molecular or ionic structure of a substance.
example: Combining vinegar and baking soda produces a fizzy chemical reaction.
note: Change of substances into new ones.

description_en: An artificial body placed in orbit around the earth or moon or another planet in order to collect information or for communication.
example: The GPS system relies on a network of orbiting satellites.
note: Includes natural moons and man-made systems.

description_en: An optical instrument designed to make distant objects appear nearer.
example: The Hubble Space Telescope has provided amazing images of distant galaxies.

description_en: Electromagnetic radiation with a wavelength shorter than that of visible light.
example: UV light is used for sterilization and identifying fake money.

description_en: A space entirely devoid of matter.
example: Outer space is nearly a perfect vacuum.
note: Empty space without air or matter.

description_en: Distance over which the wave's shape repeats.
example: Scientists can identify chemical elements by their unique light wavelengths.

description_en: The cell formed by the union of two gametes.
example: The zygote divides many times to eventually form an embryo.
note: First stage of a new organism's life cycle.
